comment_text.sn35.124:1.2: Ugga was the foremost in giving nice things (AN 1.253), a quality exemplified at AN 5.44. This Ugga (“of Vesalī”) and the Uggata of the next discourse (“of Elephant Village” near Vesalī) are both said to have eight excellent qualities (AN 8.21, AN 8.22).

root_text.sn35.124:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn35.124:1.2: Atha kho uggo gahapati vesāliko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho uggo gahapati vesāliko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.124:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme no parinibbāyanti?
root_text.sn35.124:1.4: Ko pana, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti?
root_text.sn35.124:2.1: “Santi kho, gahapati, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.124:2.2: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati. Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.124:2.5: santi kho, gahapati, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.124:2.6: Tañce bhikkhu abhinandati abhivadati ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati. Tassa taṁ abhinandato abhivadato ajjhosāya tiṭṭhato tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.124:2.8: Ayaṁ kho, gahapati, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme no parinibbāyanti.
root_text.sn35.124:3.1: Santi ca kho, gahapati, cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā, iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.124:3.2: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati. Tassa taṁ anabhinandato anabhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato na tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti, na tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.124:3.5: santi kho, gahapati, manoviññeyyā dhammā iṭṭhā kantā manāpā piyarūpā kāmūpasaṁhitā rajanīyā.
root_text.sn35.124:3.6: Tañce bhikkhu nābhinandati nābhivadati nājjhosāya tiṭṭhati, tassa taṁ anabhinandato anabhivadato anajjhosāya tiṭṭhato. Na tannissitaṁ viññāṇaṁ hoti, na tadupādānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.124:3.8: Ayaṁ kho, gahapati, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyantī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.124:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn35.124:1.2: Then the householder Ugga of Vesālī went up to the Buddha, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn35.124:1.3: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings are not fully extinguished in this very life?
translation_text.sn35.124:1.4: What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some sentient beings are fully extinguished in this very life?”
translation_text.sn35.124:2.1: “Householder, there are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.124:2.2: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them, their consciousness has that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.124:2.3: A mendicant with fuel for grasping does not become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.124:2.4: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.124:2.5: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.124:2.6: If a mendicant approves, welcomes, and keeps clinging to them, their consciousness has that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.124:2.7: A mendicant with fuel for grasping does not become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.124:2.8: That’s the cause, that’s the reason why some sentient beings are not fully extinguished in this very life.
translation_text.sn35.124:3.1: There are sights known by the eye, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.124:3.2: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them, their consciousness doesn’t have that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.124:3.3: A mendicant free of grasping becomes extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.124:3.4: There are sounds … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.124:3.5: ideas known by the mind, which are likable, desirable, agreeable, pleasant, sensual, and arousing.
translation_text.sn35.124:3.6: If a mendicant doesn’t approve, welcome, and keep clinging to them, their consciousness doesn’t have that as support and fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.124:3.7: A mendicant free of grasping becomes extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.124:3.8: That’s the cause, that’s the reason why some sentient beings are fully extinguished in this very life.”
